CREATE TABLE BusinessEntity (EntityKey INT PRIMARY KEY, Name VARCHAR(255), Credentials VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE TModel (TModelKey INT PRIMARY KEY, EntityKey INT, ModelName VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY (EntityKey) REFERENCES BusinessEntity(EntityKey));
INSERT INTO BusinessEntity (EntityKey, Name, Credentials) VALUES (1, 'Entity A', 'Cred A');
INSERT INTO BusinessEntity (EntityKey, Name, Credentials) VALUES (2, 'Entity B', 'Cred B');
INSERT INTO TModel (TModelKey, EntityKey, ModelName) VALUES (101, 1, 'Model A1');
INSERT INTO TModel (TModelKey, EntityKey, ModelName) VALUES (102, 1, 'Model A2');
INSERT INTO TModel (TModelKey, EntityKey, ModelName) VALUES (201, 2, 'Model B1');
SELECT EntityKey FROM BusinessEntity WHERE Credentials = 'Cred A';
SELECT DISTINCT t.EntityKey, b.Name FROM TModel t JOIN BusinessEntity b ON t.EntityKey = b.EntityKey WHERE b.Credentials LIKE 'Cred%';
SELECT b.EntityKey, COUNT(t.TModelKey) AS ModelCount FROM BusinessEntity b LEFT JOIN TModel t ON b.EntityKey = t.EntityKey GROUP BY b.EntityKey HAVING ModelCount > 0;
SELECT b.EntityKey, b.Name FROM BusinessEntity b WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TModel t WHERE t.EntityKey = b.EntityKey) ORDER BY b.Name;
SELECT b.EntityKey, b.Name FROM BusinessEntity b WHERE b.EntityKey IN (SELECT DISTINCT t.EntityKey FROM TModel t) AND b.Credentials <> '';
SELECT b.EntityKey, b.Name FROM BusinessEntity b LEFT JOIN TModel t ON b.EntityKey = t.EntityKey WHERE t.TModelKey IS NULL;
SELECT b.EntityKey, b.Name FROM BusinessEntity b WHERE b.EntityKey = (SELECT MAX(EntityKey) FROM BusinessEntity);
SELECT b.EntityKey, b.Name FROM BusinessEntity b WHERE b.EntityKey IN (SELECT EntityKey FROM TModel WHERE ModelName LIKE 'Model A%') ORDER BY b.EntityKey DESC;
